text.title: api-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Six recurring antipatterns when shaping API test suites.

antipattern.description: Integration test asserts only `response.status_code == 200` and the response body shape is never checked.
antipattern.reason: Status-code-only assertions miss renamed fields, dropped properties, and changed types — the most common contract regressions. A schema-validated assertion costs the same to write and catches all of them.

antipattern.description: Test fixtures define response shapes inline; the OpenAPI spec evolves; fixtures keep passing with the old shape.
antipattern.reason: Hand-crafted fixtures rot. Schema-derived fixtures (Schemathesis, hypothesis-jsonschema) stay in sync automatically. Drift produces false-positive green tests.

antipattern.description: E2E test count exceeds integration count; the suite is mostly Playwright/Cypress runs that exercise the whole stack.
antipattern.reason: Ice-cream-cone shape: tests take 10-100x longer, flake under network jitter, and obscure root cause. Push assertions down to integration with schema validation; reserve E2E for revenue-critical journeys (≤5%).

antipattern.description: Pact contracts wired between modules inside a single team's monorepo where deploys are atomic.
antipattern.reason: Pact's value is decoupling deploy cadence between teams. Inside one team, schema-validated integration tests deliver the same regression catch with a tenth of the operational overhead (broker setup, provider verification job, version negotiation).

antipattern.description: Tests rely on snapshot/diff assertions of entire response payloads instead of schema validation.
antipattern.reason: Snapshots break on every benign change (new optional field, reordered keys), pushing teams to mass `--update-snapshots` runs that bypass the original assertion intent. Schema validation only fails on real contract drift.

antipattern.description: Suite covers 2xx paths only; 4xx error-handling paths (validation, auth, rate limit) are not asserted.
antipattern.reason: The 4xx surface is where API contracts most often regress (error code renames, body shape changes). Add an integration test per documented 4xx response in the OpenAPI spec and validate body shape just as for 2xx.
